nodes.label: Cloud Native Semantic Kernel article in CompartiMOSS 56
Cloud Native AI Architecture
nodes.rationale: The article combines Semantic Kernel and Qdrant to build a fully cloud-native artificial-intelligence architecture.

nodes.label: Integrating Other LLMs with Semantic Kernel
String-encoded JSON Function Contract
nodes.rationale: Semantic Kernel functions exchange strings, so the native function returns the model response as JSON text that the caller later deserializes into a typed response.

nodes.label: Scoped Semantic Kernel Lifetime
nodes.rationale: Web applications should register the kernel as scoped because it manages state and contexts that could cause side effects when shared as a singleton.

nodes.label: Factory for Complex Plugin Initialization
nodes.rationale: A factory is recommended when native-function initialization is complex or resource-intensive.

nodes.label: Answer Score Threshold
nodes.rationale: The demo accepts answers at or above a 0.7 confidence score and returns HTTP 404 when the model cannot produce a sufficiently reliable answer.

nodes.label: Corporate ChatGPT
nodes.rationale: The proposed low-code solution gives organizational users ChatGPT-like capabilities while retaining control over consumption, access, and exposure of private data.

nodes.label: Enterprise AI Data and Access Control
nodes.rationale: Keeping AI inside controlled organizational tools mitigates security risks caused by users sending private, sensitive, or confidential information to public AI services.

nodes.label: Planning for Success with Semantic Kernel Planners
Semantic Kernel Planners
nodes.rationale: Planners use AI to select and combine registered plugin functions into steps that satisfy a user goal when developers cannot enumerate every possible workflow.

nodes.label: Action Planner
nodes.rationale: Action Planner chooses one best-aligned function, making it suitable for intent routing and low-latency scenarios.

nodes.label: Sequential Planner
nodes.rationale: Sequential Planner creates serial multi-function plans and passes each function output into the next function.

nodes.label: Stepwise Planner
nodes.rationale: Stepwise Planner dynamically explores functions and can revise its approach while solving interconnected multi-step requests.

nodes.label: Planner Relevancy Threshold
nodes.rationale: Filtering functions below a relevance threshold produces more focused and efficient plans and raises the chance of successfully satisfying complex goals.

nodes.label: Function Descriptions as Planner Metadata
nodes.rationale: Planners identify relevant functions and parameters from their descriptions, so descriptions should be verbose, concrete, complete, and unambiguous.

nodes.label: Planner Performance, Cost, and Reliability Tradeoffs
nodes.rationale: Planners are slow, make multiple costly LLM calls, and can generate defective plans because generation is stochastic; exclusions, retries, error handling, and precise descriptions mitigate these risks.

nodes.label: Agent Harness
nodes.rationale: Agent Harness forms the operational boundary where file access, tool approvals, and memory types make a capable agent governable; the model reasons, but the harness controls execution.

nodes.label: Tool Approval Strategies
nodes.rationale: Explicit auto-approved, conditional, and mandatory-human approval levels prevent sensitive or irreversible actions from executing solely on model initiative.

nodes.label: Layered Agent Memory
nodes.rationale: Separating session, operational, preference, and externally retrieved memory avoids excessive, incorrect, or unsafe retention.

nodes.label: Agent Observability
nodes.rationale: Production operation requires reconstructing user input, context retrieval, tool selection, approvals, final output, cost, latency, and failures across the complete agent flow.

nodes.label: Multi-agent Specialization
nodes.rationale: Multiple agents are justified only when each has a clear one-sentence responsibility and contract; overlapping responsibilities rapidly make the system opaque.

nodes.label: Durable Agent Workflows
nodes.rationale: Long-running processes with human waits, external calls, retries, compensations, and notifications should be modeled as durable workflows rather than one long model conversation.

nodes.label: Build and Run Agents at Scale with Microsoft Foundry
Microsoft Agent Framework at BUILD 2026 Announcement
Agent Harness: Working with Your Data Safely
